perturbation.original_text: "Confidential Technical Information" means, with respect to each Disclosing Party, any confidential Data, Trade Secrets or Technology source code within the Nuance IP or SpinCo IP, as applicable, that is in the Receiving Party's possession or that the Receiving Party obtains pursuant to the terms of this Agreement, together with any tangible or electronic expressions or embodiments thereof; provided, that "Confidential Technical Information" shall not include information that is or was (i) publicly known at the time of disclosure or thereafter without any breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Group or (ii) subsequently made known to the Receiving Party or its Group from a source unconnected with either Party or its Group.
perturbation.changed_text: "Confidential Technical Information" means, with respect to each Disclosing Party, any confidential Data, Trade Secrets or Technology source code within the Nuance IP or SpinCo IP, as applicable, that is in the Receiving Party's possession or that the Receiving Party obtains pursuant to the terms of this Agreement, together with any tangible or electronic expressions or embodiments thereof; provided, that "Confidential Technical Information" shall not include information that is or was (i) publicly known at the time of disclosure or thereafter, regardless of any breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Group or (ii) subsequently made known to the Receiving Party or its Group from a source unconnected with either Party or its Group.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the condition of 'without any breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Group' for excluding publicly known information from the definition of 'Confidential Technical Information,' we introduce a contradiction. The original definition implies that if the Receiving Party breaches the agreement and makes the information public, it remains confidential. The modified definition implies that even if the Receiving Party breaches the agreement and makes the information public, it is no longer considered confidential. This contradicts the overall intention of protecting confidential information. This change contradicts section 6.01 where it's mentioned that all confidential information of a Party disclosed to the other Party under this Agreement shall be deemed confidential and proprietary information of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 6.01. Confidentiality. Without limiting Section 6.02, all confidential information of a Party disclosed to the other Party under this Agreement shall be deemed confidential and proprietary information of the disclosing Party, shall be subject to the provisions of Section 7.09 of the Separation Agreement and may be used by the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement for the sole and express purpose of effecting the licenses granted herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that confidential information remains confidential even if disclosed due to a breach by the Receiving Party. The changed text removes this condition, stating that information becomes non-confidential if publicly known, regardless of a breach. This directly contradicts Section 6.01, which states that all disclosed confidential information 'shall be deemed confidential and proprietary information,' implying no exceptions for breaches. The modified definition creates a scenario where a breach by the Receiving Party nullifies confidentiality, contradicting the original intent and Section 6.01.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall take, at its sole expense, all reasonable measures to prevent any prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of any Confidential Technical Information, including by its Permitted Recipients, and shall be liable for any breaches of this Agreement by any of its Permitted Recipients, in each case, as if committed by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall take, at its sole expense, all reasonable measures to prevent any prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of any Confidential Technical Information, including by its Permitted Recipients, and shall not be liable for any breaches of this Agreement by any of its Permitted Recipients.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall be liable' to 'shall not be liable,' we directly contradict the original statement. The original text holds the Receiving Party responsible for breaches by its Permitted Recipients, while the modified text absolves them of this responsibility. This creates confusion regarding who is accountable if a Permitted Recipient mishandles confidential information. This change makes the agreement vague on who is responsible in case of a breach from Confidential Technical Information, creating an in-text contradiction to the original definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 6.02. Disclosure of Confidential Technical Information. Except as expressly permitted by this Agreement, including in Section 3.02(b)(vi) or Section 4.02(b)(vi), the Receiving Party shall not, and shall not permit any other Person to, disclose any Confidential Technical Information to any Person without prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, except that the Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Technical Information solely to those employees and contractors of the Receiving Party who have a need to know the Confidential Technical Information in connection with designing, developing, distributing, marketing, testing and supporting any products or services of the Receiving Party within the Nuance Field of Use or SpinCo Field of Use, as applicable (collectively, the "Permitted Recipients"); provided, that prior to such disclosure the Receiving Party shall notify each such Permitted Recipient in writing of the use and disclosure restrictions set forth in this Agreement and ensure that such Permitted Recipient is bound by confidentiality obligations with respect thereto. The Receiving Party shall take, at its sole expense, all reasonable measures to prevent any prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of any Confidential Technical Information, including by its Permitted Recipients, and shall not be liable for any breaches of this Agreement by any of its Permitted Recipients.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall be liable' to 'shall not be liable' directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement regarding responsibility for breaches related to Confidential Technical Information. The original and contradicted text state: 'The Receiving Party shall take, at its sole expense, all reasonable measures to prevent any prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of any Confidential Technical Information, including by its Permitted Recipients, and shall not be liable for any breaches of this Agreement by any of its Permitted Recipients.' The original text implied liability for the actions of Permitted Recipients, while the modified text explicitly removes that liability. This creates a strong contradiction, as both clauses cannot simultaneously be true and enforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Any assignment, other transfer or license by either Party or any member of its Group of any Intellectual Property Rights licensed to the other Party or any member of its Group pursuant to ARTICLE III or ARTICLE IV, respectively, shall be subject to the applicable licenses, covenants and restrictions set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Any assignment, other transfer or license by either Party or any member of its Group of any Intellectual Property Rights licensed to the other Party or any member of its Group pursuant to ARTICLE III or ARTICLE IV, respectively, shall not be subject to the applicable licenses, covenants and restrictions set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: By negating the condition that assignment, transfer or license by either party will not be subject to applicable licenses, covenants, and restrictions creates a clear in-text contradiction. The original text ensures that all transfers of IP rights respect the existing agreements, whereas the modified text removes this safeguard, potentially allowing transfers that violate the original intent of the licensing agreement. This change fundamentally alters the enforceability of the original licensing terms upon transfer and creates uncertainty about the binding nature of the covenants.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 8.01. No Assignment or Transfer Without Consent. Except as expressly set forth in this Agreement, neither this Agreement nor any of the rights, interests or obligations under this Agreement, including the licenses granted pursuant to this Agreement, shall be assigned, in whole or in part, by operation of Law or otherwise by either Party without the prior written consent of the other Party. Any purported assignment without such consent shall be void. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if any Party to this Agreement (or any of its successors or permitted assigns) (a) shall enter into a consolidation or merger transaction in which such Party is not the surviving entity and the surviving entity acquires or assumes all or substantially all of such Party's assets, (b) shall transfer all or substantially all of such Party's assets to any Person or (c) shall assign this Agreement to such Party's Affiliates, then, in each such case, the assigning Party (or its successors or permitted assigns, as applicable) shall ensure that the assignee or successor-in-interest expressly assumes in writing all of the obligations of the assigning Party under this Agreement, and the assigning Party shall not be required to seek consent, but shall provide written notice and evidence of such assignment, assumption or succession to the non-assigning Party. Subject to the preceding sentences, this Agreement will be binding upon, inure to the benefit of, and be enforceable by, the Parties and their respective successors and assigns. No assignment permitted by this Section 8.01 shall release the assigning Party from liability for the full performance of its obligations under this Agreement. For the avoidance of doubt, in no event will the licenses granted in this Agreement extend to products, services or other activities of the assignee existing on or before the date of the transaction described in clauses (a) or (b) of the preceding sentence, except to the extent that they were licensed under the terms of this Agreement prior to such transaction.
perturbation.justification: The change, by negating the applicability of licenses, covenants, and restrictions on assignments, directly contradicts Section 8.01, which explicitly states that neither the agreement nor any rights or obligations under it, including licenses, shall be assigned without prior written consent. The modified text allows assignments that may disregard the original licensing terms and restrictions, rendering the consent requirement and associated protections in Section 8.01 ineffective and creating a clear in-text contradiction.
